name: Light Fruitcake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Candied pineapple, cut into pieces
1 pound Red and green candied cherries
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a liner for a 10-inch tube pan by drawing a circle with an 18-inch diameter on a piece of brown paper (Do not use recycled paper). Cut out the circle and set the pan in the center, drawing around the base of the pan and inside the tube. Fold the circle into eighths, having the drawn lines on the outside. Cut off the tip end along the inside drawn line. Unfold the paper and cut along the folds to the outside drawn line.
2. From another piece of brown paper, cut a second 10-inch diameter circle; grease it and set aside. Place the first liner in the pan and grease it, then set aside.
3. Cream butter and sugar together at medium speed of an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Stir in vanilla and lemon extracts.
4. Beat egg yolks separately. Alternately add egg yolks and 3 cups flour to the creamed mixture, mixing gently after each addition.
5. Combine candied fruit, raisins, and nuts with 1/2 cup flour. Stir to coat well. Add this mixture to the batter and fold in gently.
6. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form; fold into the batter using a spatula or whisk.
7. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and arrange additional fruit and nuts on top, if desired. Cover the pan with the greased 10-inch brown paper circle, placing the greased side down.
8. Bake at 250 degrees Fahrenheit for 4 hours or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove from the oven and carefully remove the paper cover.
9. Slowly pour 1/4 cup brandy evenly over the cake; let cool completely on a wire rack.
10. Remove the cake from the pan, peel off the paper liner, and wrap the cake in the brandy-soaked cheesecloth. Store in an airtight container in a cool place for up to 3 weeks. Pour a small amount of brandy over the cake each week to keep it moist. For longer storage, refrigerate the fruitcake; it will slice more neatly and evenly.
